title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) in Statistics & Data Science
content: This is a four year degree program thoughtfully designed for curious minds who enjoy solving real-world problems through numbers, logic, and technology. It offers students a strong grounding in statistical theory while also opening doors to the exciting world of modern data science. From understanding the fundamentals of probability and inference to diving into machine learning, big data, and artificial intelligence, this program ensures a well-rounded academic journey. What sets it apart is its hands-on approach—students learn to code in Python and R, explore data using industry-relevant tools, and apply their skills to meaningful projects and internships. The program is flexible, with a wide variety of electives ranging from Natural Language Processing and Cloud Computing to Healthcare Analytics and Quantum Computing, allowing students to tailor their learning to their interests and career goals. Whether you see yourself building AI systems, analyzing trends in finance, solving healthcare challenges, or pursuing cutting-edge research, this program gives you the tools, the freedom, and the confidence to get there. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list. 01 This research-embedded program empowers students to lead independent statistical research on complex datasets, validate predictive models via advanced methods, produce publishable dissertations, and drive interdisciplinary collaborations in data sciences. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 Graduates will secure research or analyst positions in academia/R&D firms, publishing at least 2 peer-reviewed papers on statistical methods or data science applications within 5 years. 02 Graduates will create new ideas in data research. They will lead projects that use advanced methods like Bayesian inference and deep learning. 03 Graduates will mentor juniors or contribute to open-source data tools, achieving recognition through conferences or grants in statistics/data sciences. 04 Graduates will transition to doctoral programs or senior roles, demonstrating research independence and ethical handling of complex datasets. 01 Statistical and Mathematical Knowledge: Demonstrate in-depth knowledge of statistical theories, data analysis techniques, and computational tools essential for extracting meaningful insights from data, and apply this knowledge effectively across diverse real-world domains. 02 Data Science and Computational Skills: Apply the knowledge of data science tools and programming languages (e.g., R, Python, SQL) to manage, analyse, and visualize large datasets using techniques such as machine learning, data mining, and statistical modeling. 03 Real-World Applications: Formulate real-world problems into statistical and data-driven models, and design appropriate analytical approaches, algorithms, or computational solutions to interpret data and support informed decision-making. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list.
